content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 19, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 19, 2026, focused on stabilizing the Eliza Cloud infrastructure following a production incident, decommissioning legacy routing services, and enhancing the dedicated agent experience. Significant progress was made in database connection management, cloud-native authentication, and mobile build reliability, alongside a comprehensive documentation sync across the framework.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cloud Infrastructure & Routing**
*   Decommissioned BitRouter in favor of a Cloudflare Worker-based gateway, simplifying LLM routing and improving reliability ([#8741](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8741), [#8738](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8738)).
*   Implemented Cloudflare KV cache backend to resolve connectivity issues with Railway Redis ([#8665](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8665)).
*   Optimized database connection pooling for dedicated agents to prevent client exhaustion incidents ([#8695](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8695)).
*   Added round-robin load balancing for Cerebras API keys to mitigate rate-limiting issues ([#8735](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8735)).

**Agent Orchestration & Features**
*   Introduced parity features for Codex/Claude-Code, including MCP auto-inheritance and sub-agent nesting ([#8740](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8740)).
*   Replaced TensorFlow.js with a native ggml YOLOv8n detector in the vision plugin to resolve build warnings and improve performance ([#8725](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8725)).
*   Added "LEAN_CHAT_PLUGINS" preset to reduce cold-boot times for dedicated conversational agents ([#8730](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8730)).

**UI & Mobile Experience**
*   Streamlined the first-run experience by auto-skipping runtime pickers on cloud-only hosts ([#8742](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8742)).
*   Implemented a sign-in agent picker allowing users to choose existing cloud agents or create new ones ([#8721](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8721)).
*   Fixed mobile build regressions, ensuring web assets are correctly bundled into Android APKs ([#8387](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8387)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   **Orchestration & Core:** [#8751](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8751) (re-attach app-deploy contract), [#8750](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8750) (fix task failure narration), [#8749](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8749) (bound planner loop), [#8746](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8746) (resolve model names), [#8745](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8745) (SSRF address pinning).
*   **Plugins & Apps:** [#8694](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8694), [#8693](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8693), [#8692](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8692), [#8691](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8691) (new AppView surfaces for various plugins).
*   **Maintenance:** [#8747](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8747) (dependency bumps).

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Cloud Scaling & Infrastructure**
*   Addressed production connection exhaustion and database architecture scaling ([#8696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8696), [#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321)).
*   Resolved billing leaks and correctness issues, including atomic credit decrements and container stop logic ([#8342](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8342)).

**Mobile & Platform Stability**
*   Fixed Android build regressions where web assets were missing from APKs ([#8387](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8387)).
*   Resolved pre-launch gaps regarding iOS signing, onboarding, and wallet DeFi ([#8652](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8652)).

**Agent & Connector Logic**
*   Fixed issues where the VIEWS action hijacked text-connector turns ([#8613](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8613)).
*   General improvements to coding agent orchestration ([#8124](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8124)).

## NEW ISSUES

**Cloud & Scaling**
*   [#8621](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8621): Tracked the mobile/desktop cloud agent experience post-squash.
*   [#8696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8696): Addressed database connection architecture and pooler requirements following production incidents.

**Collaboration & Integration**
*   [#8699](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8699): Invitation for the Nautilus platform regarding A2A protocol infrastructure.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

*   **[#8621](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8621) Mobile/desktop cloud agents:** Discussions confirmed the implementation of unified cloud-side authentication for dedicated agents. The team successfully moved away from dedicated-specific auth code in the app, opting for a proxy-based approach that auto-resumes agents. Remaining work involves app-side wiring and operator-level KV repointing.
*   **[#8696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8696) Scale: dedicated-agent DB connection architecture:** The team mitigated immediate connection exhaustion via env-tunable pools. Long-term plans involve implementing transaction-mode pooling (PgBouncer/Supavisor) and splitting control-plane databases from agent-state databases.
*   **[#8342](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8342) Apps billing leaks:** All critical leaks (suspended container stops, deprovisioning, and billing idempotency) have been resolved. Pricing models for isolated databases were decided, opting to fold costs into container fees rather than separate line items.
